segments.title: Ideal destroyers depend on era, but screening stays constant
segments.summary: The opening frames the ideal destroyer as era-dependent while keeping screening and task-group protection as the through-line.
segments.body: This is a useful organizing segment for destroyer coverage: the transcript separates offensive firepower from the more persistent role of protecting fleets, task groups, and ships from large and small threats.

evidence.note: Opening thesis explains that ideal destroyers change by decade but remain centered on screening and protection.

segments.title: 1920s destroyer crews as the backbone of later capability
segments.summary: The 1920s section treats destroyer crews and experience as the practical backbone for later destroyer capability.
segments.body: The discussion partitions the interwar portion of the episode, where destroyers are not just hull counts but a professional base for later fleet screening.

evidence.note: The 1920s section treats destroyer crews and experience as the practical backbone for later destroyer capability.

segments.title: Destroyer air defence depends on guns and stable platforms
segments.summary: The middle section explains why destroyer air defence needs appropriate guns and stable firing platforms.
segments.body: This separates the anti-aircraft and gunnery argument from the broader screening thesis.

evidence.note: The middle section explains why destroyer air defence needs appropriate guns and stable firing platforms.

segments.title: Modern destroyers become heavy generalist screen ships
segments.summary: The episode identifies the shift toward destroyers as heavy generalists while keeping screening as the background requirement.
segments.body: The discussion connects Cold War and modern destroyer design to the older escort-screening role.

evidence.note: The episode identifies the shift toward destroyers as heavy generalists while keeping screening as the background requirement.

segments.title: Burke evolution as a long-running destroyer path
segments.summary: The Burke discussion frames the class as an evolved answer to persistent destroyer requirements.
segments.body: This section helps users compare continuity and growth in modern destroyer programs.

evidence.note: The Burke discussion frames the class as an evolved answer to persistent destroyer requirements.

segments.title: Maya and modern fleet-screening endpoints
segments.summary: The later section uses Maya and other modern destroyers to close the argument about screening fleets today.
segments.body: This gives the video a modern endpoint for users browsing Japanese, Burke-like, and high-end screen ships.

evidence.note: The later section uses Maya and other modern destroyers to close the argument about screening fleets today.
